evidence: Each frame carries one focus: the prompt card alone, then the candidate/meter/score trio anchored by the growing arrow, then the centered walk-home question card.
At most three or four elements appear together (card, meter, candidate arrow, score readout), well under the budget.
'candidate' sits beside the arrow, 'meter' under the gauge, and 'score' is the readout itself; no boxed legend appears.
On-screen text is confined to labels and short prompt cards ('retrieval prompt', 'for the walk home / same direction, smaller size?', '3 ways to continue').
All text including 'direction unchanged' and the score readout is comfortably readable at frame resolution.
All cards, the meter, the candidate arrow, and readouts sit fully inside the frame with generous margins.
No text or labels overlap; the arrow, score, meter, and cards occupy separate regions.
Candidate/observed is periwinkle blue and the climbing score is lilac, consistent with token semantics across frames.
No mascots or ornaments appear; the intervening blank frame is a transition rather than decorative filler.

The prompt card holds while the meter idles, the periwinkle candidate stretches longer as its lilac score climbs to 4.80 with 'direction unchanged', and the walk-home card plus '3 ways to continue' chip close the beat.
The 'direction unchanged' note sits near the candidate arrow and the score sits under the meter it drives.
Across frames the candidate arrow lengthens and the score climbs as a continuous progression rather than a cut.
Elements are spaced with ample negative space and a clear left-to-right reading order.
